home.sessionBestInline: Session record: {{count}} correct in a row
home.modesSubtext: Pick how you want to study — Practice is the best place to start.
home.calendarCardSubtitle: Meetings and absences for your enrollments
home.categoryHint: Modes below use your class unit {{unit}} when available. Category pills set global defaults for declension practice.
home.streakNudge: Practice today to keep your {{count}}-day streak going.
home.goalsHint: Set optional session goals and see your daily streak in Settings.

settings.title: Settings
settings.subtitle: Game preferences and account
settings.sessionGoalsHelp: Optional targets for Practice mode (time and form-count goals are alternatives—pick one type).
settings.uiLanguageHelp: English or Russian for buttons and menus. Learning content stays the same.
settings.faqUrlBody: When you enroll in a class, practice links use your class and unit so progress lines up with your teacher’s curriculum. Overview and flat routes still work if you are not in a class.

analytics.title: Class analytics
analytics.unitFilterHint: Some older records have no unit_id; they are included when you choose "All units" but not when you filter to a single catalog unit.
analytics.noCurriculumScope: This class has no visible curriculum rows yet; stats use all mastery for enrolled students.
analytics.rosterHint: Higher attention = lower mastery, fewer recent sessions, or longer time since last activity. Not a grade.
analytics.weeklyActivityHint: Sessions and question counts are proxies for engagement, not exact study time.

studentDetail.classScopeBanner: Showing mastery and sessions aligned with class curriculum: {{className}}.
studentDetail.cohortCompare: Class median average mastery (visible units): {{cohort}} · This student: {{student}}

progress.title: Your progress
progress.subtitle: Topic-level mastery and suggested next steps based on your recent practice.
progress.noTopics: No topics yet — join a class or complete a practice session to see data here.
progress.disclaimer: Suggestions are heuristic hints from your practice data. They do not predict exam scores.
